items.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.
items.author: Jeffrey Schwartz
items.content: <article>IBM and Red Hat assign 20,000 engineers to the new Project Lightwell service as Anthropic's Mythos findings ignite debate over how to secure the open-source software supply chain.</article>

items.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability.</article>

items.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule.</article>

items.title: When Too Much Security Data Became the Risk
items.author: Joan Goodchild
items.content: <article>Rapid growth turned routine firewall logs into a security and budget liability. One CISO used artificial intelligence to filter what data truly belongs in the SIEM.</article>

items.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>LLMs consistently hallucinate Web domains for legitimate brands that attackers can register for malicious activity in a difficult-to-detect attack vector.</article>

items.title: Safe Events Start With Threat Intel & Digital Security
items.author: Olga Polishchuk
items.content: <article>Planning ahead to defend against cyber threats is the work that keeps events uneventful.</article>

items.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor.</article>

items.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>"Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions.</article>

items.title: Attackers Seize Exposed AI Endpoints to Power Offensive Ops
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Threat actors don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is.</article>

items.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
items.author: Kristina Beek
items.content: <article>In this "Heard it From a CISO" video, Silverfort CISO John Paul Cunningham explains that AI in cybersecurity workflows is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to break into this essential field.</article>

items.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.</article>

items.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
items.author: Yelena Mujibur Sheikh
items.content: <article>Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.</article>

items.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.</article>

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.</article>

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.</article>

items.title: Can Clothes Make You Invisible to Facial Recognition?
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>Does life feel Orwellian sometimes? One researcher has a solution for you: graphic tees that confuse the neural networks in surveillance cameras.</article>

items.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.</article>

items.title: Amazon Q VS Extension Flaw Leads to Cloud Credential Theft
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>Adversaries could plant a malicious repository that can execute arbitrary code and steal cloud credentials by exploiting the vulnerability, which showcases growing MCP risk.</article>

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
items.author: Bree Fowler
items.content: <article>Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.</article>

items.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>Companies are still experimenting with automated AI systems to find security weaknesses, but fewer are relying on the technology.</article>

items.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
items.author: Jeffrey Schwartz
items.content: <article>Cisco joins a growing list of security platform providers that are betting that securing the agentic workforce means turning identity into the primary control plane.</article>

items.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
items.author: Arielle Waldman
items.content: <article>The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance.</article>

items.title: AI Won't Wipe Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs
items.author: Jon France
items.content: <article>Instead of eliminating jobs for early-career cyber pros, AI is creating new opportunities for candidates with strong human decision-making skills.</article>

items.title: Meeting Trump's 2030 Quantum Deadline Will be Expensive, Complex
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Getting accurate visibility into IT and OT systems will be compounded by multivendor environments, misaligned update life cycles, and interoperability gaps.</article>

items.title: Thanks for Crushing the Submissions Inbox. We're Trying to Keep Up
items.author: Becky Bracken
items.content: <article>It might be taking a bit longer than usual to respond to your submissions — here's why.</article>

items.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
items.author: Ericka Chickowski
items.content: <article>The fintech company's engineering-first application security team reengineered the process for granting system access, making it easier and more secure for developers working on their projects. Here are the lessons learned from Robinhood's experience.</article>

items.title: In Less Than 24 Hours, Attackers Weaponize Cisco CUCM Flaw
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>The flaw enables server-side request forgery (SSRF) and escalates privileges to root, impacting Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM SME deployments.</article>

items.title: Russian APT 'Gamaredon' Upgrades Its Arsenal, Requiring New Defenses
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>The FSB state-sponsored operation has gotten a lot better at loading its malware and hiding its servers.</article>

items.title: EdTech Attackers Shift From Schools to Their Software Suppliers
items.author: Arielle Waldman
items.content: <article>Educational institutions, the edtech companies they rely on, and, more concerningly, the challenges they pose for schools are the focus of the latest Reporters' Notebook video series.</article>

items.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>With tens of billions of dollars flowing into regional economies from cybercrime, scam centers continue to flourish, despite international and law-enforcement efforts.</article>

items.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.</article>

items.title: Attackers Hit Cisco SD-WAN Flaw 2 Months Before Disclosure
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>Researchers believe rogue peering was used to connect to the victim's SD-WAN devices to gain admin privileges and root-level access.</article>

items.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico.</article>

items.title: Do CISOs Need a Code of Ethics?
items.author: Dark Reading Editorial Team
items.content: <article>Dark Reading Confidential Episode 19: Kickbacks, no-show jobs, "dirty" VCs, and shelf ware — industry expert Robert "RSnake" Hansen explains why he thinks it's time for a CISO code of ethics. It could ensure cybersecurity bosses aren't engaged in self-dealing that could risk enterprise, and even national, security.</article>

items.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>OpenClaw removed five packages from its ClawHub skills marketplace that bypassed security checks even though they included infostealers and other threats.</article>

items.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.</article>

items.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data.</article>

items.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>The CI/CD workflow weakness affects Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris analytics database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python Software Foundation's Black.</article>

items.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.</article>

items.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.</article>

items.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.</article>

items.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
items.author: Elizabeth Montalbano
items.content: <article>Attackers are using multiple online channels — including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal — to build an illusion of trust to spread a cross-platform clipboard hijacker.</article>

items.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
items.author: Arielle Waldman
items.content: <article>Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.</article>

items.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
items.author: Robert Lemos
items.content: <article>As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis.</article>

items.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
items.author: Jai Vijayan
items.content: <article>A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.</article>

items.title: Operation Escaneo Signals Shift in LatAm Threat Landscape
items.author: Alexander Culafi
items.content: <article>The threat group's curious business model may combine opportunistic monetization alongside intel collection, without much coordination between the two.</article>

items.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>A hacker could have "Rickrolled" the World Cup — or worse — thanks to FIFA's unenforced Entra access controls.</article>

items.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
items.author: Rob Wright
items.content: <article>Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.</article>

items.title: Get Out of Security Debt by Tackling the Exposure Problem
items.author: Chris Wysopal
items.content: <article>Teams digging out of security debt need to answer only two simple questions: Which vulnerabilities in our systems are exposed, and how long should they stay that way?</article>

items.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
items.author: Nate Nelson
items.content: <article>"Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.</article>
